title: Live Convergence Mode
subtitle: Watch as you run: cumulative means settle and the distribution grows from a sparse bar chart into a bell shape. Each completed run adds a point, helping you answer the "how many runs is enough?" question.
convergenceHint: ✓ Stable: cumulative mean changed by less than {{percent}}% over the last {{window}} runs. You can stop here.
convergenceWaiting: Not yet converged: the cumulative mean is still moving — keep accumulating samples.

chart.xMaxLabelTooltip: Capital N = the total replications you planned to run (set in Settings → Batch). The chart extends to this value.
chart.hint: Orange line: cumulative mean. Light-orange band: 95% CI. The band tightens as 1/√n.

help.summary: ℹ️ How to read this chart (cumulative-mean convergence)
help.xAxis: X axis = which run we are at. Each completed run adds a point.
help.yAxis: Y axis = the average of the metric across runs 1..n so far.
help.elementOrangeLine: Orange line: the cumulative mean. It updates after every run.
help.elementBand: Light-orange band: 95% CI. The true value sits inside this range with 95% probability. Band shrinks as samples grow.
help.elementBlip: Pulsing orange dot at the right: the most recent run.
help.elementGrayDashed: Gray dashed horizontal line: run 1's lone value. Useful for comparing one run vs many.
help.elementRedLine: Red horizontal line: the current cumulative mean.
help.elementGreenZone: Light-green region: where the curve was detected as stable.
help.howRead1: First ~30 runs swing wildly → result not yet trustworthy.
help.howRead2: Band tightens steadily → answer becomes more precise (1/√n shrinkage).
help.howRead3: Once the green stable zone appears (last 100 runs change < 1%), you can stop.
help.howRead4: If run 1 (gray dashed) is far from the current mean (red line), it proves a single run can mislead.
help.howRead5: Hover any point on the curve to see that run's exact mean and CI.
help.notationN: **Capital N** = total runs you planned (e.g. Target N=50). Set in Settings → Batch replications.
help.notationLowerN: **Lowercase n** = runs completed so far (e.g. n=42 means 42 runs done). Grows as the batch progresses.
help.notationWhyDifferent: Mid-run (e.g. 30/50): N=50, n=30. After completion they match (n=N=50).

histogram.title: {{metric}} distribution
histogram.totalCountTooltip: Lowercase n = the number of samples collected so far (= replications completed).
histogram.hint: Each completed run drops a sample into a bin. Sparse at low n; bell-shaped as n grows.

help.summary: ℹ️ How to read this chart (distribution)
help.xAxis: X axis = the metric's value range (low to high).
help.yAxis: Y axis = how many runs landed in each value range. Tall bar = common value.
help.elementBars: Orange bars: the histogram, splitting samples into 20 bins.
help.elementKde: Dark-orange smooth curve: KDE (kernel density estimate) — the smoothed continuous shape. The more samples, the smoother it gets.
help.elementMedian: Black dashed 'Median': 50% of runs lie to the left, 50% to the right.
help.elementP5P95: Gray dashed P5 / P95: the 5% and 95% quantiles. The middle 90% lives between them (range shown top-right).
help.elementMeanRed: Red dashed 'mean': the cumulative mean. Far from the median ⇒ skewed distribution.
help.elementShape: Bottom-right 'Distribution shape': automatic skewness/kurtosis verdict in plain language.
help.howRead1: Bell-shaped (symmetric) → near-normal, results are concentrated and predictable.
help.howRead2: Right-skewed (long tail right) → occasional very large values (waiting times often look this way).
help.howRead3: Left-skewed (long tail left) → occasional very small values.
help.howRead4: Long tail → heavy outliers, treat carefully (many random processes do this).
help.howRead5: Mean far from median → distribution is skewed, looking only at the mean is misleading.
help.howRead6: Hover any bar to see its count, density, and total n.
help.notationN: **Capital N** = total runs you planned (set in Settings → Batch replications).
help.notationLowerN: **Lowercase n** = samples collected so far (= rows feeding this histogram).
help.notationWhyDifferent: While running, N > n (e.g. target N=50, current n=30 means 20 left). After completion n=N.

bootstrap.subtitle: Batch starting. The cat animation begins as soon as run 1 completes; the cumulative-mean curve grows alongside.
bootstrap.runningHint: Cat SVG animation will start in a moment

scenePanel.title: Current Run Animation
scenePanel.thumbnailHint: Click any chip to switch the animation to that run
scenePanel.collapse: Collapse the scene panel; charts go full-width
scenePanel.replay: Restart this run's animation from the beginning

smallMultiples.title: Multi-metric live panel
smallMultiples.hint: Every completed run adds a point to all charts at once. Click any to drill in.
smallMultiples.configureHint: Pick at least 1. Your selection is remembered.

replay.title: Redraw all curves growing from n=1 to the end

emptyState.title: No batch run yet
emptyState.lead: Live Mode is for asking "after N runs, has the answer settled?"
emptyState.needBatch: Drawing the cumulative-mean curve and the distribution shape needs **at least 2 runs**.
emptyState.singleRunHint: For a single run, use Playback → 🎓 Learning Mode instead — those cards (event clock, queue length, Little's Law, utilization) are real-time signals *inside* one simulation.
emptyState.howToStart: How to start a batch run
emptyState.step3: Set the run count (N=20 minimum; N=50 shows convergence well)
emptyState.step4: Hit Run — the app will bring you back here automatically
emptyState.quickStartBatch: 🚀 Run a 20-replica batch with current params
emptyState.quickStartHint: Or kick one off right now (reuses your last config):
